mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes Citan CDI engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM607 engine typically costs £2,500–£5,000 supply only, with used units from £1,500–£3,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. A timing belt replacement costs £400–£800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Citan.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes Citan CDI (OM607 / K9K) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM607 (Renault K9K) is a durable, proven diesel when properly maintained. It has a potential lifespan of 200,000+ miles with regular servicing. However, it has non‑negotiable service items — the timing belt must be replaced at the manufacturer‑specified interval. Injector, DPF, EGR and turbo actuator issues are common but manageable with regular maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Citan CDI engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing belt neglect (catastrophic if it snaps), injector failure (rough idle, smoke, power loss), DPF clogging (short‑journey use), EGR valve clogging, turbocharger actuator issues, and hard starting (glow plugs). The engine is fundamentally robust when properly maintained.

mainEntity.name: Does the Citan CDI have a timing belt or chain?

acceptedAnswer.text: It has a timing belt — not a chain. Mercedes and Renault specify replacement at the manufacturer‑specified interval — many specialists recommend changing the belt, tensioner and water pump together every 80,000–100,000 miles or 5–7 years. If the belt snaps, full engine replacement is almost always required.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Citan CDI engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and overall condition. A Citan CDI is a £3,000–£12,000 vehicle — a £3,500 recon is 29–117% of the car's value. For lower‑value examples (sub‑£4,000), replacement may exceed our 70% Mercedes scrap threshold. A well‑maintained, lower‑mileage example justifies replacement; a tired, high‑mileage one may not.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes Citan CDI engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The Citan CDI engine code is OM607 (K9K) — a 1.5‑litre four‑cylinder turbo diesel. Later Euro 6d‑Temp versions use OM608. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes Citan CDI Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current OM607/OM608 generation. Used engines from low‑mileage donors are available from around £1,500. A reconditioned engine from a specialist typically costs between £2,500 and £5,000 supply only.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Citan CDI engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The 1.5 dCi engine is relatively straightforward to work on.

cta.label: → Compare Citan CDI engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Belt Replacement (All Citan CDI — Critical Maintenance)
problems.symptoms: No symptoms before failure — catastrophic engine damage occurs if the belt snaps. Engine cuts out, piston‑valve contact, complete engine failure.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (timing belt, tensioner and water pump kit)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the service interval is due or overdue, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The OM607 uses a timing belt, not a chain. If the belt snaps, piston‑valve contact is almost certain, requiring a full engine replacement. Many specialists recommend replacing the belt, tensioner and water pump together every 80,000–100,000 miles or 5–7 years. This is the single most important maintenance item on the Citan CDI — ignoring it is a gamble that can cost you a £2,500+ engine replacement.

problems.title: Diesel Injector Failure (All Citan CDI)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hard starting, loss of power, increased fuel consumption, excessive smoke (black or white), engine stalling, check engine light.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (injector set reconditioning or replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: Injector failure is one of the most commonly reported issues on the OM607. Symptoms include rough running, hard starting and excessive smoke. If left unaddressed, a failing injector can cause damage to the DPF and catalytic converter. A diesel injector cleaning service can often restore performance; if not, reconditioned or new injectors are available. Using high‑quality diesel fuel and periodic injector cleaning are essential for long‑term reliability.

problems.title: DPF Clogging and Exhaust Backpressure Sensor Failure
problems.symptoms: Reduced engine power, excessive smoke, check engine light, failed DPF regeneration, strong exhaust smell.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (DPF forced regeneration or clean); £150–£400 (exhaust backpressure sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The Citan CDI is prone to DPF clogging, particularly if used for short journeys. A faulty exhaust backpressure sensor is a common cause of DPF issues. Regular motorway runs allow the DPF to regenerate passively. If the check engine light appears, have the DPF system inspected — a forced regeneration or sensor replacement is far cheaper than a complete DPF replacement.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Citan CDI — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Routine maintenance — do preventatively at 80k–100k miles
Recondition or replace — cheaper than engine damage
Forced regeneration or clean — regular motorway runs prevent it

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Citan CDI? Check These First
buyIf: * Timing belt replaced within the last 80,000 miles or 5 years
* No rough idle or smoke — injectors in good condition
* Full Mercedes service history with correct oil spec
avoidIf: * Timing belt overdue with no replacement history — walk away or budget for it
* Rough idle with no injector history — injectors may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — DPF or EGR issues likely
* Poor service history — OM607 engines are sensitive to neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The OM607 1.5 dCi is a durable, proven diesel when properly maintained — but it has non‑negotiable service items. The timing belt must be replaced at the manufacturer‑specified interval; ignoring this is a gamble that can cost you a £2,500+ engine replacement. Injector, DPF, EGR and turbo actuator issues are common but manageable with regular maintenance. Buy on full service history with evidence of timing belt replacement — and never ignore a rough idle or check engine light.

cta.label: → Compare Citan CDI engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes Citan CDI (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Timing Belt History — Replaced within 80,000–100,000 miles or 5–7 years? (Critical)
* Rough Idle — Check for uneven running; injector failure indicator
* Smoke Check — Black or white smoke can indicate injector, EGR or turbo issues
* Service History — including engine era (OM607/OM608) and confirmed oil‑change intervals
* DPF Status — Check for warning lights; short journeys can cause clogging
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling or hissing; turbo actuator issues possible
* Starting — Check for hard starting; glow plug or injector issues possible
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly. The same engine family is used across all three badges with different tuning states.
